Xilog Maestro - Editor Друк етикеток
Закриває програму. SCM Group Xilog Maestro. Натисніть, щоб відкрити, зберегти, закрити проект. Для отримання додаткової інформації натисніть F1. Вихід Нещодавні файли
Головна Файли Буфер обміну Створити Імпорт Вид Навігація Дисплей Вибір Undo-Redo Зберегти Зберегти як Зберегти як проект Save as sub-program Save as technology Save as global variables Відкрити Імпортувати Імпорт скрипта Import piece Імпорт dxf Імпорт Pgm Імпорт tcn Закрити Print Про нас Посібник Вирізати Копіювати Вставити Скасувати Новий Проект Nesting Mix Piece Top Left Right Front Rear Bottom Selected SW isometric view S isometric view SE isometric view Pan Orbit Zoom Zoom Zoom + Zoom - Dynamic Zoom Piece Zoom Каркас Без прихованих ліній Затінення 25% Затінення 50% Затінення 75% Непрозорий вид Render view CAM Опції Opaque View Hide Geometries start Hide Processes start Hide machining attributes Hide all attributes Hide depth attributes Hide speed attributes Hide head rotation attributes Hide X rotation attributes Hide roll stop attributes Hide edge supply attributes Hide edge banding attributes Hide edge rollers open attributes Hide edge rollers close attributes Hide lamp switch on attributes Hide workpiece name Hide blocking rules Hide surfaces Blocking states Machining encumbrances Redraw Select Undo Redo
Креслення Geometries Line Length and angle line Infinite straight line Semi-infinite straight line Bisector Arc Arc by radius Arc by centre Arco datum centre and 2 points Arc by angle Arc tangent continue Circle by centre Circle by radius Circle by diameter Circle Circle by 3 tangent points Circle by 2 tangent points and radius Ellipse Ellipse by centre and semi-axes Elliptical arc by centre and semi-axes Ellipse by centre and axes Elliptical arc by centre and axes Ellipse contained in a rectangle Polyline Spline Polygon Polygon by side length Rectangle Rectangle by dimensions Point Text Slot Polygon given the radius Polygon given the side Flat surface Linear extrusion Revolution Sweeping Scored mesh Corner mesh Section surface Extend surface Intersection between surfaces Cut surface Adapt piece to surfaces Projection Projection on plane Normal projection to surface Polyline3D Arc 3D Wrapping Import surface Import from STL Import from image Extract edges from surface Modify Fillet Chamfer Trim Extend Offset Join Explode Repetitions Rectangular R Series C Series Clamping profile Move Rotate Scale Mirror Mirror in relation to line Project Measure Micro-fasteners Depth Speed Edge banding attributes Head rotation Rotation X Edge supply Lamp switch on Roller stop Machining start Machining end Edge rollers open Edge rollers close Insert part Insert by geometry Osnap Auto Final Intersection Quadrant Midpoint Perpendicular Tangent Centre Parallel Point Horizontal Vertical Near Off Multiple Grid On Connector Parameters Grid Angled planes Slice 2 lines Generic Parallel From geometry From surfaces Surfaces 3D криві Change surfaces Solids
Операції Boring Routing Machinings 3D Edge banding Modiffy Boring Slanted drilling Routing Channel Cut with blade Pocket forming Perimeter Routing Dowelling Nailing Profile track feeling Corner cleaning Slanted routing Chamfering 3D routing Roughing 3D pocketing Finishing Swarfing Milling on surface Tool path wrap Set reference point Edge banding End trimming Trimming Edge scraper Glue scraper Radius Starting point Technology Technology Select technology Apply technology XControl XControl Micro-fasteners Attributes Clamping rules Labelling
Macro
Edge banding optimisation Edge banding machining optimiser Allows the execution of optimisation commands for edge banding. Edge banding optimisation command. Trimming machining optimiser Allows the execution of optimisation commands for trimming. Trimming optimisation command. Check Simulation Simulation Allows the execution of the simulation command for edge banding machining. Simulation command. Production process Production process Constructs a complete sequence of the edge banding machining with properties defined by the user to apply to specific geometries. Production process command. Hide collision points Used to disable the edge banding head collision points display. Hide collision points.
Detected table programming with Setup. Confirming with the command the Setup programming will be reset
Detected table programming with EPL. Confirming with the command the EPL programming will be reset Setup Закрити Закрити Close the piece availability context on the work top. Закрити Movement Suction device rotation Enables rotation of the suction device. Suction device rotation Fix Bar Allows the movement just of the support, keeping the bar to which it belongs in fixed position. Fix Bar Fix Support Allows the movement just of the bar, keeping the relative supports in fixed position. Fix Support Clamp at limit switches When the clamp is moved to a position incompatible with the position of the adjacent phases, because over the limit switches, its movement is blocked. Clamp at limit switches Alignment Supports align Align the supports selected. Supports align Operations Initial set-up Generates the initial set-up phase, useful to position the piece on the work top. Initial set-up Final setup Generates the final setup phase, which is used when removing the piece from the work plane. Final Setup Cycles generate Generates the cycles to run to position the supports and the bars in the way specified by the user in the work phases. Cycles generation Automatic Setup Start automatic work plane supports positioning. Automatic Setup Cycles cancel Cancels all project cycles. Cycles cancellation Collisions Collisions check Start the working plane supports collisions check. Collisions check Show collision areas Shows the collision areas for the work table in the current phase. Show collision areas Check blocking coherence Check that the blocking devices are sufficient for each workpiece and that they block the workpiece adequately. Check blocking coherence Check programming Check that the table has been programmed correctly. Check table programming Pincering Pincering rules Pincering rules selection Allows to select the rules that define the pincering mode of the clamp on the piece. Load distance The clamp is in the position that guarantees loading of the piece to be worked. Load distance Minimum pincering The clamp blocks the minimum section of the piece, which guarantees sufficient blocking. Minimum pincering Maximum pincering The clamp blocks the maximum section possible of the piece. Maximum pincering Maximum gripping without pickups The clamp blocks the maximum possible piece section that guarantees an exchange without pickups. Maximum gripping without pickups Devices cancel Cancels the blocking device taking the work top back to the initial parking condition. Devices cancellation Cancel current cycle devices Cancels the current cycle blocking device taking the work top back to the initial parking condition. Cancellation of the current cycle devices Devices park Parks the blocking device taking the work top back to the initial parking condition. Devices parking Current cycle devices park Parks the current cycle blocking device taking the work top back to the initial parking condition. Parking of the current cycle devices Display Central bars Central mobile bars Enable/disable the use of the central mobile bars.\nWhen the button is pressed, the central mobile bars are disabled. The mobile bar {0} cannot be activated.\n Detected collision with another machine element. One or more blocking bar is out of the area. Table setup Save table layout Save the type and layout of supports on the motor-driven tables, so that they can be loaded automatically. Save table layout
Parametric
Machines NC functions ISO Null operation Print message Parking Table cleaning Switching the blower on Probing Special probing Piece probing rotation translation Piece (internal) probing rotation translation Combiflex N.C. functions Pickup quota Tools Tooling Environment variables Edges E.P.L. Create layout Cancel layout Post Post Project check Project time estimate Projects list time estimate Tool path Simulazione lavorazioni Configuration Configuration Supports Machine Elements Setup Setup Delete settings Optimiser Optimiser Guided optimisation Basic Optimisation Wizard Advanced Optimisation Wizard Automatic Optimisation Tools file update
Tools
Help Options Options Settings Backup Restore
Macro Macro Management Display macro Show tools Create macro Create component Import macro
Advanced Advanced project wizard
Edgebanding programs Edgebanding programs
Materials store Materials store Materials unloading Remnants loading
Profile management Feedrate profiles Create feedrate profile Display feedrate profile Technology tables Create technology table Display technology table Selected feedrate profiles will be deleted.Is not possible to cancel the operation.\nProceeed? Selected technology tables will be deleted.Is not possible to cancel the operation.\nProceeed?
Nesting Report Nesting Report
Authentication Enter password Change password New password Confirm password
Update Update project on which to print the labels Control used to update the project on which to print the labels loading it from the directory Fine Gruppo File-->
Label printing Single printing Single label printing. Control that allows printing the selected single label. Print all Print all labels. Control that allows printing all the labels on the sheet. Status reset Labels status reset. Control that allows the reset of all the labels on the sheet. Print selection Print labels selected. Control used to print the labels selected in the graphic. Print all labels in the project? Not all labels in the project have been selected for printing. Continue loading the project? The folder used to load the projects to print is empty.
Top face Bottom face Left face Right face Front face Rear face Save current project before closing it? Warning Name Value Tool properties Lines Circles Cartesian point Arc of a circle Polylines Geometric data Absolute coordinates of Solved value Select geometry All information Angle ° Cut references Top reference Bottom reference Distance from reference Anti touch (°) Generate additional plane Material To right of geometry To left of geometry Columns centre-to-centre Reference Ellipses Arc of a circle (mm) (inches) [m/min] (feet/min)
X Y Z Selection Zoom up Zoom down Pan Orbit Dynamic Zoom Window Zoom Centre point End point Horizontal Intersection Midpoint Nearest point Point Disabled Parallel Perpendicular Quadrant Tangent Vertical Multiple Millimetres Inches
Information. For displaying information about the application, such as version, copyright, licence. Guide Used to open the application manual Options. For modifying application options, such as file folders, machining data, the PostProcessor, etc. Backup settings. Enables the user to create a backup of the application settings by saving them to a file. Restore settings. Enables the user to restore the application settings by uploading them from a file or by using the default settings. Advanced project wizard. Command used to open the Wizard to create a project with the possibility of optimising the machining and define the table positioning. Edgebanding programs. Control used to open the controller to create, change and update the fields used to specify the edgebandign programs to associate to the piece label. Materials store. Command used to open the material store controller. Materials unloading. Command used to unload the aterials used from the store. Remnants loading. Command used to load the product remnants in the store. Profiles management. Speed profiles manager used to create, change and delete the speed profile. Create profile. Command used to create the speed profile. Display profile. Displays the speed profiles available and allows the execution of operations such as change, delete, copy, etc. Technology tables management. Speed technologies tables manager, used to create, change and delete the technology table. Create table. Command used to create the table of the speed technologies. Display table. Displays the speed technology tables available and allows the execution of operations such as change, delete, copy, etc. Nesting Report. Control used to open the controller to create Nesting results. Exit. Closes the application. For further information, press F1. New CAM Project. For creating a new CAM project in which geometries can be drawn, machining applied, etc. New Nesting Project.
For creating a new Nesting project using a special Wizard with step by step guidance for defining sheets, parts and parameters.Nesting may be Rectangular or FreeForm.
New project mix.
Enables the user to create a new project mix, i.e. a set of projects for each one of which it is possible to specify the number of repetitions, blocking type, plane type, machining area, etc.
New piece.
For creating a new piece of the parallelepiped type, or by extrusion in Z of a drawn geometry.
Save (Ctrl + S). Saving of the current file. Save project with name. Save with name of a CAM project. Save macro with name. Save with name of a macro. Save technology. Save with name of a technology. Save global variables. Save with global variables name. Open file (Ctrl + O). Opening of a file. Закрити. Closing of the current project. Import Control Allows the execution of the workpiece import commands, script file, file dxf. Import piece. Allows the execution of the workpiece import commands, script file, file dxf. Import script. For importing the content of a script file. Import dxf file. Allows to import the geometries of a dxf file. Import Pgm file. Allows to import a program in Pgm format. Import tcn file. Allows to import a program in tcn format. Paste (Ctrl + V). For pasting the content of the Clipboard. Cut (Ctrl + X). For removing geometric elements and/or machining and moving them to the Clipboard. Copy (Ctrl + C). For copying geometric elements and/or machining to the Clipboard. Delete (Canc). For deleting the selected geometric elements and/or machining. Top view. For viewing the top surface of the piece. Bottom view. For viewing the bottom surface of the piece. Left view. For viewing the left surface of the piece. Right view. For viewing the right surface of the piece. Front view. For viewing the front surface of the piece. Rear view. For viewing the rear surface of the piece. View selected. Allows to place the display in orthogonal mode to the work top/face currently selected in the menu. Axonometric view SW. Allows to put the display in South-West Axonometric view. Axonometric view S. Allows to put the display in South Axonometric view. Axonometric view SE. Allows to put the display in South-East Axonometric view. Extension Zoom. For performing a Zoom which allows a view of all content in the graphical area. Window Zoom. For performing a Zoom using a rectangle for selecting the area to be zoomed. Zoom In. For Zooming in, by clicking on the left mouse key in the area to be zoomed. Zoom Out. For Zooming out, by clicking on the left mouse key in the area to be zoomed. Dynamic Zoom. For a dynamic Zoom action by clicking on the left mouse key in the area to be zoomed, moving with the mouse. Pan. For moving the view of the graphics by clicking on the left mouse key and moving with the mouse. Orbital view. For rotating the view of the graphics by clicking on the left mouse button and moving with the mouse. Wireframe view. Enables the view showing the hidden lines. Wireframe without hidden lines. Enables the view without showing the hidden lines. Shading 75% view. Enables the shading view with 75% transparency. Shading 50% view. Enables the shading view with 50% transparency. Shading 25% view. Enables the shading view with 25% transparency. Opaque view. Enables the actual view of the piece machined in shading mode. Render view. Enables the actual view of the piece machined in texture mode. Display options Allows to select the options to apply to the display. Opaque view. Enables the view only of the machining present on the current surface. Hide initial geometries point. Allows to disable the view of the initial points of the geometries. Hide initial processing point. Allows to disable the view of the initial points of the processes. Hide edge banding attributes. Used to disable the display of attributes applied to edge banding machining. Hide attributes. Allows to disable the display of the attributes applied to the machinings. Hide all attributes. Disables the display of all the attributes applied to the machinings. Hide depth attributes. Disables the display of the depth attributes applied to the machinings. Hide speed attributes. Disables the display of the speed attributes applied to the machinings. Hide head rotation attributes. Disables the display of the head rotation attributes applied to the machinings. Hide rotation X attributes. Disables the display of the rotation X attributes applied to the machining. Hide roller stop attributes. Disables the display of the roller stop attributes applied to the machining. Hide edging supply attributes. Disables the display of the edging supply attributes applied to the machining. Hide edge rollers open attributes. Used to disable the display the open attributes of the rollers that hold the edge. Hide edge rollers close attributes Used to disable the display the close attributes of the rollers that hold the edge. Hide lamp switch on attributes. Disables the display of the lamp switch on attributes applied to the machining. Hide workpiece name. Disables the display of the workpiece name. Hide blocking rules. ows to disable the display of the blocking rules. Hide surfaces (Alt + S). Used to disable the surfaces display. Display blocking states Enables/Disables the display of the blocking status of the work top clamps. Machining encumbrance Enables/disables the display of the machining encumbrance. CAM view. Enables the view of the tool path. Redraw. Updates the drawing in the graphical area. Select. Enables command for selection of geometries and/or machining in graphical area. Undo Enables the command that can be used to return to the state before the last action. Redo Enables the command that can be used to return to the state following the last action. Line by 2 points. Enables the command for drawing a line using 2 points. Line angle length. Enables command for drawing a line given the starting point, length and angle of inclination relative to the X axis. Arc by 3 points. Enables the command for drawing an arc passing through 3 points. Arc by radius. Enables command for drawing an arc given the starting point, end point and radius. Arc by centre Enables command for drawing an arc given the starting point, end point and centre. Centre datum arc, radius and angle. Enables the command that can be used to draw a centre datum arc, a point that defines the radius and the angle at the centre. Arc tangent continue. Enables the command for drawing an arc tangent to a geometry. Circle by 3 points. Enables the command for drawing a circle passing through 3 points. Circle by centre and radius. Enables the command for drawing a circle given the centre and the radius. Circle by centre and diameter. Enables command for drawing a circle given the centre and diameter. Ellipse data centre, semi axis and angle. Enables the command used to draw an ellipse data centre, semi axis and angle. Arc of ellipse data centre, semi axis and angle. Enables the command used to draw an arc of ellipse data centre, semi axis and angle. Polyline. Enables the command for drawing a polyline, i.e.: a continuous sequence of arcs and/or straight lines, open or closed. Rectangle. Enables the command for drawing a rectangle given 2 diagonal vertices. Rectangle by dimensions. Enables the command used to draw a rectangle by centre and dimensions. Slot.
Enables the command for drawing a slot, i.e.: a rectangle whose vertices are filleted. It is drawn from the centre, the length of the smaller semi-axis, the length of the larger semi-axis and the angle of inclination.
Polygon by centre and radius.
Enables the command used to draw a regular polygon by number of sides. The drawing is performed from the centre of the polygon, the radius of the circle circumscribed and the tilting angle.
Polygon by centre and side.
Enables the command used to draw a regular polygon by number of sides. The drawing is performed from the centre of the polygon, the side of the polygon and the tilting angle.
Cartesian point. Enables the command for drawing a Cartesian point. Text. Enables command for drawing a text, defining its style, position and size of characters. Flat surface. Enables the command used to construct a flat surface starting from a closed 2D curve. Surface for linear extrusion. Enables the command used to construct a surface for linear extrusion starting from a curve on a plane. Revolution surface. Enables the command used to construct a revolution surface starting from a curve on a plane and an axis. Sweeping surface. Enables the command used to create a surface for extrusion of a 2D curve (section) along a guide curve. Scored mesh surface. Enables the command used to construct a scored mesh starting from two curves. Corner mesh type surface. Enables the command used to construct a corner mesh starting from four adjacent curves. Surface from sections. Enables the command used to construct a surface starting from a series of sections. There must be at least three sections and they must be specified in the exact order that they must be interpolated. Extend surfaces. Enables the command used to extend a surface by a given length. Import 3D models consisting of surfaces. Enables the command used to import one or more surfaces contained in files IGES, STEP, etc. Import 3D objects from STL file. Enables the command used to import a 3D object described by a STL file. Import 3D objects from image file. Enables the command used to import a 3D object reconstructed from an image. Intersection between surfaces. Enables the command used to intersect two surfaces. The command creates, along the intersection curves, an equal amount of 3D curves. Cut of a surface along a curve. Enables the command used to split a surface along a curve along the surface. If the curve is not on the surface, it would be automatically projected on the surface. The final curve must be closed or in any way mark a closed portion of surface. Adapt piece to surfaces. Enables the command used to redefine the dimensions of the piece so that it contains exactly one or more surfaces selected. Projection of a curve on surfaces. Enables the command used to create a 3D curve projecting a planar curve on a surface. Projection of a curve on the current plane. Enables the command used to create a 2D curve, by projecting a 3D curve on the current plane. Projection of a curve perpendicular to a surface. Enables the command used to create a 3D curve by projecting a curve almost perpendicular to a surface: each point of the start curve is projected perpendicular to the target surface; the resulting curve passes through these points. Wrap of a curve on surfaces. Enables the command used to create a 3D curve wrapping a planar curve on a surface. Edges from surface extraction. Enables the command used to create in one go the 3D curves that represent the edges of a surface. Polyline 3D. Enables the command used to create a polyline 3D constructed by linear sections. Arc 3D Enables the command used to create a 3D arc through 3 points. Fillet. Enables the command for creating a fillet on 2 geometric elements. Chamfer. Enables the command for creating a chamfer on 2 geometric elements. Extend. Enables the command that can be used to extend a geometrical element (segment or arc) so that it intersects another. Trim. Enables the command for trimming parts of geometric elements. Join.
Enables the command for joining geometries which have a common point, to form a polyline.
Explode. Enables the command for exploding a polyline into separate geometries. Offset. Enables command for generating a geometry offset by a desired value relative to the reference one. Repetitions. Enables the command used to create a repetition of geometries and/or machining. Rectangular repetition. Enables the command used to create a rectangular repetition of geometies and/or machinings. Clamping profile. Enables the command used to draw the workpiece clamping profile.\nThe geometry that represents the clamping profile must be drawn so that the workpiece is always on its left.\nThe offset indicated is the minimum clamping. Move. Enables the command for moving a geometry and/or machining operation. Rotate. Enables the command for rotating a geometry and/or machining operation. Scale. Enables the command used to scale a geometry and/or a machining. Mirror. Enables command for generating a geometry and/or machining operation which mirrors the reference one. Mirror in relation to a line. Enables the command used to generate a geometry and/or a machining mirrored in relation to any line, starting from the one given as reference. Project. Enables the command for generating a geometry and/or machining operation on the opposite surface to the reference surface. Starting point. Enables the command for modifying the starting point of a geometry. Measure. Enables the command used to measure the distance between two points. Micro-fasteners. Enables the command used to manually create the micro-fasteners. Depth. Enables the command used to create a depth type attribute. Speed. Enables the command used to create a speed type attribute. Head rotation. Enables the command used to create a edgebanding head rotation attribute. Rotation X. Enables the command to create a rotation attribute in relation to axis X. Roller stop. Enables the command used to create a edgebanding roller stop attribute. Edging supply. Disables the command used to create an edging supply attribute. Lamp switch on. Enables the command used to create a lamp switch on attribute. Move machining start. Enables the command used to move the machining start point on the associated geometry. Move machining end. Enables the command used to move the machining end point on the associated geometry. Edge rollers open. Enables the command used to create the open attribute of the rollers that hold the edge. Edge rollers close Enables the command used to create the close attribute of the rollers that hold the edge. Reference system generic work top.
Enables the command for generating a plane from its clockwise reference system, entering the values of the origin and the 2 angles of rotation relative to the Z and X axes of the absolute reference system.
Parallel plane. Enables the command for generating a plane parallel with the one currently selected. Plane from geometry.
Enables the command that can be used to generate a plane through a geometry, and slanted at an angle with respect to the normal geometry plane .
Plane from surface
Enables the command used to generate a plane starting from a flat surface.
Disabale Osnap. (F2) Disables osnaps in the drawing. Final osnap (F6). Enables osnap to the final point of a geometry. Intersection osnap (F9). Enables osnap to the point of intersection of one or more geometries. Midpoint osnap (F7). Enables osnap to the midpoint of a geometry. Centre osnap (F8). Enables osnap to the centre point of a geometry. Quadrant osnap (F5). Enables osnap to the quadrant point of a geometry. Perpendicular osnap (F11). Enables osnap perpendicular to a geometry. Tangent osnap (F10). Enables osnap tangent to a geometry. Parallel osnap (F12). Enables osnap parallel with a geometry. Point osnap. Enables osnap to a point of a geometry. Horizontal osnap. Enables osnap horizontal to a geometry. Vertical osnap. Enables osnap vertical to a geometry. Close osnap (F4). Enables osnap to the point nearest to a geometry. Multiple osnap. Enables one or more osnap at the same time. Enables the osnap in the final point of a geometry in the multiple osnap. Enables the osnap in the intersection point of one or more geometries in the multiple osnap. Enables the osnap in the mid point of a geometry in the multiple osnap. Enables the osnap in the central point of a geometry in the multiple osnap. Enables the osnap quadrant point of a geometry in the multiple osnap. Enables the osnap perpendicular to a geometry in the multiple osnap. Enables the osnap tangent to a geometry in the multiple osnap. Enables the osnap parallel to a geometry in the multiple osnap. Enables the osnap in a point of a geometry in the multiple osnap. Enables the osnap horizontal to a geometry in the multiple osnap. Enables the osnap vertical to a geometry in the multiple osnap. Enables the osnap in the point close to a geometry in the multiple osnap. Boring.
Command for creating one or more bored holes. Boring can be created using tables or by selecting one or more geometries of a point or circle type.
Tilting drilling.
Command used to create one or more tilting drilling operations. The drilling can be created in the table by selecting one or more point or circle geometries.
Routing.
Control that allows to create one or more milled parts. Milling is applied onto one or more geometries of any type, except for the point.
Channel.
Control that allows to create one or more channels. The channel can be created in a table or by selecting a linear type geometry.
Cut with blade.
Control that allows to create one or more cuts with blade. The blade cut can be defined in a table or by selecting a linear type geometry on the upper work top of the piece.
Emptying.
Control that allows to create one or more emptyings.\n Emptying can be performed by selecting the contour geometry of the emptying and the geometries that define the islands, i.e. the areas of material not to work.
Contouring.
Control that allows to create contouring. The contouring can be performed on the geometry of the piece and on an internally or externally closed geometry at it.
Profile track feeling. Command used to create a track feeling of a profile. The profile track feeling can be made on the piece geometry and on any open or closed type of geometry. Corner cleaning. Control used to create a corner cleaning. The corner cleaning can be performed on the end of a poly-line between two segments that form a corner of 90 degrees. Slanted routing.
Command that can be used to create a slanted routing. The routing is applied to one or more geometry of any type other than a point, defined on the upper plane or a plane lying parallel to the upper face. The machining makes it possible to executing routing with a tool that is slanted with respect to the normal working plane.
Chamfering.
Command used to create a chamfering. The chamfering is applied to one or more geometries of any type, except for the point, defined on the upper table or an a table parallel to the upper side. The machining allows routing with tool tilted in relation to the worktable normal.
3D routing.
Command that can be used to create a routing in the space. The routing is defined over a linear tract in the three-dimensional space using two rotation angles of the tool at the approach point, with respect to the X and Z axes.
Roughing.
Command used to rough a surface. Roughing can be applied to one or more surface at the same time, creating a single tool path that, in subsequent passes, removes as much material as possible from the raw workpiece.
Finishing.
Command used to finish a surface. Finishing can be applied to one or more surface at the same time, creating a single tool path that, in parallel passes, removes excess material from a roughed workpiece.
Swarfing
Command used to create surface machining with tool side. The swarfing can be applied to two curves (upper and lower guide) in relation to which the tool slides: it can be applied also to one or more surfaces
Milling on surface.
Command used to create a milling on a surface. The milling is applied to a curve on a given surface, in relation to which the tool orientation during machining will be specified.
Tool path wrap.
Command used to apply a 3 axis tool path on a surface. The tool path will be wrapped on a specified surface; the tool orientation will be calculated based on the perpendicular to the surface.
Change reference point for roughing or finishing.
Command used to create or change the reference point of a roughing or finishing. The reference point is used to change the start point of the machining selected.
Edge banding. Command used to create one or more edge banding. The edge banding is applied to one or more geometries SoftForming edgebanding. Control used to create one or more SoftForming edgebanding. The edging is applied to one or more geometries. End trimming. Command used to create one or more end trimmings. The end trimming is applied on an edge banding Trimming. Command used to create one or more trimmings. The trimming is applied on an edge banding Edge scraper. Command used to create one or more scraping. The scraping is applied on an edge banding Glue scraper. Command used to create one or more scraping. The scraping is applied on an edge banding Radius. Command used to create one or more radiuses. The radius is applied on an edge banding Dowelling. Control that allows creating one or more dowelling machinings. Nailing. Control that allows creating one or more nailing machinings. Technology selection.
Control that allows to select the technology of a process to apply to a geometry and/or an existing process.
Technology application.
Control that allows to apply the technology of a process to a geometry and/or an existing process.
CN ISO function. Enables the control that allows to insert the CN ISO function. CN XN function. Enables the control that allows to insert the CN XN function. CN XMSG function. Enables the control that allows to insert the CN XMSG function. CN PARK function. Enables the control that allows to insert the CN PARK function. Table cleaning NC function. Enables the command used to insert the NC function that enables the extractor hood to clean the table. NC function of blower switch-on. Enables the command to switch the blower on prematurely to carry out the hot blowing. CN Probing Function. Enables the command that can be used to insert the CN probing function. Special probing NC function. Enables the command used to insert the Prisma special probing NC function . Workpiece rotation translation probing NC funtion. Enables the command used to insert the probing NC function of the piece rotation and translation. Workpiece rotation translation (internal) probing NC funtion. Enables the command used to insert the (internal) probing NC function of the piece rotation and translation. Workpiece pickup quota Ussed to define the information for the workpiece oickup by the picker. EPL Cancel EPL layout.
Enables the control that allows to start the work tops editor for the configuration of the bars, the clamps and/or the work top suction devices.
Enables the control that allows to cancel the configuration of the bars and the supports created as work top in the work tops editor.
Post Processor. Enables the control that allows to send the translator towards the Xilog language. Project check.
Control that allows to check whether the current project contains errors that would not allow the execution in the machine with the current configuration.
Times estimate results Save results Export results Закрити Current project time estimate.
Command used to estimate the current project execution time.
Projects list time estimate.
Command used to estimate the projects list execution time.
Tool path display Enables the command used to execute the tool path viewer Visualizzatore della simulazione delle lavorazioni. Abilita il comando che permette di visualizzare la asportazione del materiale da parte degli utensili. Set-up.
Enables the control that allows to open the layout context of the pieces on the work top for each individual work phase, on the basis of the type of work top, bars and clamps and/or suction devices that are available.
Delete settings
Remove the information about the machine elements from the data base phases.
Tools editor.
Enables the control that allows to start the tools editor, in which it is possible to create, modify, cancel the work tools.
Tools on the machines tooling editor. Enables the command used to start the tools tooling editor, where the tools can be associated to the head spindles and tool changes. Tools file update wizard Enables the command used to start the Wizard to update the tools file in a project, and update the invalid machinings. Environment variables.
Selection of the file containing the environment variables. Selecting the file the environment variables will be loaded in the project.
Edge editor.
Enables the command that can be used open the edge editor, where it is possible create, modify and delete edges.
Configuration editor.
Enables the control that allows to start the configuration editor, in which it is possible to create, modify machine configuration.
Supports editor.
Enables the control that allows to start the supports editor, in which it is possible to create, modify, cancel the library supports.
Machine elements editor. Enables the control that allows to start the machine elements editor, in which it is possible to create, modify, cancel the machine elements from the library. Optimisation control Allows the execution of the optimisation control for technological sequence and tool pathway. Guided optimisation. Optimisation managed via an options window that allows order of the processes, tools associated to a process, expansion of sub-programs and the choice also to optimise the tool pathway. Advanced optimisation wizard. Optimisation for Nesting projects managed with an options window used to order by machinings and the possibility of generating machining units. Add Remove Units Add or Remove machining units to the list of machinings to optimise. Automatic optimisation. Optimisation realised via the choice of the sub-programs expansion, enabling the optimisation of the tool pathway, disabling the technological sequence. XControl component test XControl component test Adding Nesting parts.
Enables the user to add a part to the Nesting sheet, uploading it from a file and positioning at the desired point with a given rotation.
Adding Nesting parts by geometry. Enables the user to add a part to the Nesting sheet, by geometry profiles and positioning at the desired point with a given rotation. Macro management
Macro manager used to define from the ones available the one to activate, delete, change, or create new Macros.
Display macro.
Displays the macros currently loaded and used to execute operations such as selecting which ones to activate, delete, check etc.
Show groups. Shows the groups in the interface that contain the Macros and to execute operations such as the creation, deletion, change the groups and the Macros contained. Create macro.
Used to open a Wizard to create a Macro, in which the parameters that compose it can be defined, their type, their description, associate images to identify the Macros etc
Create component.
Opens a Wizard to create a component, in which you can specify the reference macro, the parameters that must continue and be displayed and the default value of the hidden parameters.
Import macro.
Used to import an old format Macro in a Maestro format Macro.
Creation of a remnant or scrap.
Used to create a remnant or scrap of nesting starting from a geometry that defines the countour.
Offset Repetitions Right/left offset

The geometries that are compatible with slotting are segments and polylines with all the tracts in the same direction . Slotting cannot be applied to any other type of geometry.
The geometries that are compatible with saw cutting are segments and polylines with all the tracts in the same direction . Saw cutting cannot be applied to any other type of geometry.
The geometries that are compatible with pocketing are closed geometries such as circles, ellipses and ploylines. Pocketing cannot be applied to any other type of geometry.
The geometries that compatible with contouring are closed geometries such as circles, ellipses and ploylines. Contouring cannot be applied to any other type of geometry.
The geometries compatible with the dowelling are Cartesian points and circles. Dowelling cannot be applied to any other type of geometry. The geometries compatible with the nailing are on the Cartesian point. It is not possible to apply nailing on geometries that are not the point.

Bevelling cannot be applied on the Cartesian point.

Automatic optimisation Optimization enabled Step Options Generic Along X Along Y Rows Columns Optimization type Disabling technological sequence Cancels the obligation to maintain the technological sequence when the tool change is optimised. Tool run optimisation Searches for the minimum pathway that the tool can run following processing. Sub-programs optimisation In the phases tree, replace the sub-program with the list of its processes. In this way, tool change is optimised(or for tool pathway) also on the sub-program processes. Machining merge optimisation Optimise the cuts that can be made from one workpiece to the next with just one machining, if various workpieces are laid out on the worktable. Invert tool path Used to invert the tool path (only for routing)
Guided optimisation Advanced Wizard Optimization Date Tool Processes Tools Work phases All phases All processes belonging to the project phases are displayed; the process sequence will be a reference for all phases during the optimisation. Work sheets All sheets All processes belonging to all project sheets are displayed; the process sequence will be a reference for all sheets during the optimisation. Add Delete Copy Paste Name Type of parameter Type of value Physical unit Default value Visibility Multiple values Null value Unit Description Image
Identification name not found in the parameter corresponding to the parameter name in the Execute function of the Macro. It cannot be empty or contain spaces.
A parameter can have different meanings, it can be user type, associated to the workpiece dimensions , it can be tool type etc.
A user type parameter can assume one of the canonical mathematical values such as decimal, integer, string, Boolean etc.
Physical unit of the parameter, it can be adimensional such as an angle or a rotation speed, or it can be a length, or a machining feed speed.
Indicates if the decimal, integer or Boolean user parameter can assume the null value.
Default value of the parameter when the Macro is loaded to be used.
Indicates if the parameter must be visible to the user to be changed or if it must be hidden for internal use.
Definition of the couples name value that can be matched with the parameter.
Identifies the parameter location when the Macro is loaded, if the field is empty it will be located in the General data, otherwise it will be inserted in a special unit of the identification name not location specified.
Identificaiton of the parameter description that will be displayed in the Tooltip when the Macro is loaded. Image associated to the parameter when the Macro is loaded. Optimization Edge banding: type of optimization Edge banding parameters optimization Optimizes the approach to avoid collision with the head, the edge dispensing parameters and the infeed and outfeed wait times using the data of the technology of the edge assigned to the edge banding. Optimization in outfeed Optimization in outfeed Optimization in closure Active only for closed edgebandings, enable the use of the edge bander head end trimmer to cut the edging in closure. Optimisation of the Autostart Disable the Autostart machine instruction for the first edge banding enabling it for the subsequent edgings. Optimisation of the speed Optimisation of the speed Optimisation of the rotations Calculate the edge bander head rotations along the profile to avoid collisions using the data of the edging technology assigned to the edgebanding. Optimisation of the lamps Calculate the switch on/off of the lamps along the profile using the data of the edging technology assigned to the edgebanding. Optimisation of the roller stop Calculate the insertion of the roller stop along the profile using the data of the edging technology assigned to the edgebanding.

Tooling optimiser PowerSet Tooling optimiser. It opens a Wizard used to create an optimisd tooling. MIX project (*.mixx)|*.mixx Tooling (*.atrx)|*.atrx Tooling (*.atr)|*.atr Machine configuration (*.cfgx)|*.cfgx Tools database (*.tlgx)|*.tlgx Simple return execution backwards of the forward movement Tooling optimiser PowerSet Back Forward Run Stop Закрити Parametri di ottimizzazione Allows the setting of the optimisation criteria and options for the tooling optimisation. Execution Allows a tooling optimisation operation to be executed or stopped. Results Allows access to the tooling optimisation operation results. Analysis criteria Create a new tooling Integrate existing tooling Check existing tooling Improve existing tooling Toolings Programs mix Tools database Use current tools database Machine configuration Use current machine configuration PowerTool path Optimisation options
Tooling analysis, starting from an empty tooling, to create a tooling that minimises the execution time. An analysis procedure is started that analyses all the programs in the execution list that, starting from an empty tooling, creates a tooling configuration to execute all the programs as quickly as possible.
Tooling analysis, starting from a loaded tooling, to integrate the selected tooling with the drills necessary to minimise the execution time. An analysis procedure is started that analyses all the programs in the execution list that, starting from a loaded tooling, integrates the current composition, without replacing the drills already present, to minimise the programs execution time
Loaded tooling analysis to assess the execution time of a list of programs. An analysis procedure is started that analyses all the programs in the execution list that, using the tooling loaded, produces a report with the execution times and the number of stops obtained for the programs. The loaded tooling is not changed.
Analisi attrezzaggio, a partire da attrezzaggio caricato, per modificare l'attrezzaggio selezionato con le punte necessarie a minimizzare il tempo di esecuzione. Viene avviata una procedura di analisi di tutti i programmi in lista di esecuzione che, partendo da un attrezzaggio caricato, modifica la composizione corrente, aggiungendo e sostituendo eventuali punte, per minimizzare il tempo di esecuzione dei programmi. L'ottimizzatore fornisce una serie di soluzioni relative ad numero crescente di sostituzioni punte (0, 1, 2, ecc.) e relativi tempi di esecuzione, demandando all'utente la scelta dell'attrezzaggio più opportuno da utilizzare.

Select / deselect all updates The results contained in this document, as well as all the indications supplied by this optimizer, are calculated by considering panels made of wood or similar materials, with coatings or ennobling that comply to modern market standards. If the panels have different characteristics from those mentioned, it may be necessary to modify production parameters such as, for example, travel speed or drilling speed, and as a consequence, productivity results may be lower than those calculated. In particular, use of delicate coatings results in a decrease in productivity that can be only be determined empirically. I risultati (numero di step, fermate e ribaltamenti del pannello) potrebbero differire da quelli ottenibili in macchina, perché l'ottimizzatore di ciclica è sviluppato da altro fornitore. OPTIMIZED PROJECT Rimozione automatica sfridi Attivando questa opzione, al termine di ogni lavorazione, viene automaticamente rimosso ogni sfrido generato: in tal caso, fra le parti generate dalla lavorazione verrà mantenuta solo quella di volume maggiore. Esplodi ripetizioni Controlla il bloccaggio con un solo morsetto Quando abilitato, controlla che, se esiste una colonna di pezzi bloccata con un solo morsetto, anche le altre colonne siano bloccate con un solo morsetto. Porzione minima in [mm] del pezzo per la cuffia Porzione minima in [mm] del pezzo che deve essere pressata con la cuffia. Nesting Tube Ordina automaticamente le lavorazioni per il cambio utensile Quando abilitato, le lavorazioni vengono ordinate automaticamente prima dell'ottimizzazione, in modo da eseguire per prime quelle più vicine alla testa del pannello e caricare in modo mascherato gli utensili corrispondenti. Se questa funzionalità viene disabilitata, il primo utensile caricato in modo mascherato è quello della prima lavorazione che si trova all'interno del programma.
